message:
feat(tests): Add real-behavior test harness with skip protocol (bd-f97)

- Add test_skip.bash: Structured skip handling with actionable messages
  - require_command, require_env, require_file checks
  - Actionable next steps for each skip reason
  - Skip summary at end of test run

- Add test_exec.bash: Command execution logging with diagnostics
  - Captures stdout/stderr/exit code/duration
  - Environment variable capture (with secret redaction)
  - Stream separation verification
  - Full debug dump on failure

- Update test_harness.bash: Integrate new modules
  - Initialize exec logging in setup
  - Print skip summary in teardown

- Add test_harness_demo.sh: Sample tests demonstrating usage
  - 15 passing tests showing skip protocol, exec logging, stream separation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-8cg","title":"Update CLI contract + schemas for new commands","description":"# Update CLI Contract + Schemas for New Commands

## Purpose
Keep the CLI contract and JSON schemas authoritative as new commands/features land (status/report, prune, repos validate/discover, config migrate, fallback output).

## Scope
- Update `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` with new subcommands, flags, and example JSON payloads.
- Add/extend schema files:
  - `schemas/status-details.json`
  - `schemas/report-details.json` (or reuse status if identical)
  - `schemas/prune-details.json`
  - `schemas/repos-details.json`
  - `schemas/config-details.json`
  - `schemas/fallback-details.json`
- Ensure the envelope schema includes `schema_version` (additive) and stays backward compatible.
- Align error codes and stream-separation guidance for the new commands.

## Design Notes
- Prefer additive schema changes (no breaking removals).
- Keep names consistent (`status` vs `report`) across docs, schemas, and CLI help.
- Include explicit `run_id`, `duration_ms`, `warnings`, and `errors` fields in all new schemas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI_CONTRACT documents status/report, prune, repos validate/discover, config validate/migrate, and fallback outputs.
- [ ] New schema files exist and validate against example fixtures.
- [ ] Schema changes are additive and compatible with existing envelope.
- [ ] Examples include both success and error cases for each new command.

## Test Plan
- [ ] Add fixtures for each new schema and validate with `test_json_schemas.sh` (ajv/jq paths).
- [ ] CI validates schema changes with strict checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:49:46.083055419Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:05:36.575837209Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:05:36.575789839Z","close_reason":"Created new schema files (status-details.json, prune-details.json, repos-details.json, config-details.json, fallback-details.json), updated envelope.json with new commands and schema_version field, created test fixtures, and updated CLI_CONTRACT.md with documentation for status, prune, repos validate/discover, and config migrate subcommands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8cg","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:37.780347948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-9vb","title":"E2E integration test: dsr check command","description":"# E2E Integration Test: dsr check Command

## Command Purpose
`dsr check` detects throttled GitHub Actions workflow runs by monitoring queue times.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Basic Functionality
- `dsr check` with no repos returns helpful message
- `dsr check --repos ntm` checks specified repo
- `dsr check --all` checks all registered repos
- `dsr check --threshold 300` uses custom threshold

### 2. JSON Mode Output
- `dsr check --json` returns valid JSON
- JSON matches check-details.json schema
- Contains repos_checked, throttled, healthy arrays
- Exit code reflected in envelope

### 3. Exit Codes
- Exit 0 when no repos throttled
- Exit 1 when any repo throttled
- Exit 3 on gh auth failure
- Exit 4 on invalid arguments

### 4. Flag Combinations
- `--quiet` suppresses non-error output
- `--verbose` shows detailed API calls
- `--non-interactive` disables prompts
- `--json --quiet` outputs only JSON

### 5. Edge Cases
- Unknown repo name produces error
- Network failure handled gracefully
- Rate-limited API returns retry hint
- Empty workflow runs list handled

## Test Approach
- Use real dsr binary
- Test against a test repo OR skip network tests
- Verify JSON schema compliance
- Capture and verify exit codes
- Use temp config dir for isolation

## Logging Requirements
- Log test name and description
- Log command executed
- Log expected vs actual output
- Log pass/fail with timing","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:15.818315831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:12.865396967Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:46:34.938101688Z","close_reason":"CONSOLIDATED: Merged into bd-1jt.5.3 (integration tests for dsr commands)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9vb","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:15.818315831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-9vb","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:12.864689495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-dy5","title":"E2E: dsr prune (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr prune (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify prune respects safety rules and only deletes within allowed dsr state paths.

## Approach
- Use temp state dirs with dummy artifacts/logs.
- Run `dsr prune --dry-run` and real prune.
- Ensure safe delete refuses non-dsr paths.

## Logging
- Print prune plan and deleted paths.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and directory tree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dry-run lists intended deletions.
- [ ] Real prune removes only allowed files.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid and stderr clean on success.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:34.343178800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:01.101622931Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dy5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:58.920513238Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dy5","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:01.100860484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-f97","title":"Test harness: real-behavior logging + skip protocol","description":"# Test Harness: Real-Behavior Logging + Skip Protocol

## Purpose
Standardize how tests log commands, capture stdout/stderr, and skip when external deps are missing, without resorting to mocks.

## Scope
- Shared helpers that:
  - Capture stdout/stderr on failure.
  - Print the exact command and environment.
  - Emit a structured skip message with actionable install steps.
- Enforce stream separation checks in tests.

## Requirements
- All unit/E2E tests import the same harness helpers.
- Skips must include a **reason** and **next step** (e.g., "Install minisign" or "Run gh auth login").
- Never suppress errors silently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A reusable helper exists for log capture + skip reasons.
- [ ] Sample tests show consistent logging format.
- [ ] Failures dump stdout/stderr and relevant env vars.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:54:37.594231147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:01:47.829161040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-iu8","title":"Write README.md user guide for dsr","description":"# Write README.md User Guide for dsr

## Purpose
Provide a clear, single entry-point doc for users and operators so dsr can be installed, configured, and used without reading internal plans.

## Must Include
- What dsr is + when to use it (GH Actions throttling)
- Quickstart: `dsr config init`, `dsr repos list`, `dsr check`, `dsr build`, `dsr release`, `dsr fallback`, `dsr doctor`, `dsr status`
- Config + registry layout (XDG + `repos.d/`)
- Build host expectations (trj/mmini/wlap) + toolchains
- Installer strategy + security notes (checksums/minisign)
- Exit codes table
- Logging/state locations and how to inspect failures
- Non-interactive + JSON modes (stream separation)
- Links to `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` and `docs/ACT_SETUP.md`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] README exists and is consistent with CLI contract
- [ ] Examples are copy-pasteable and current
- [ ] No contradictions with docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md
- [ ] Links to docs and config locations are  {"id":"bd-ov2","title":"Test infrastructure: coverage reporting and metrics","description":"# Test Infrastructure: Coverage Reporting and Metrics

## Purpose
Provide function-level and line-level coverage estimation for Bash scripts without external tools.

## Approach
Since Bash doesn't have built-in coverage tools like gcov, we use:
1. **Function enumeration**: Parse exported functions from each module
2. **Test mapping**: Track which tests call which functions
3. **Execution tracing**: Optional PS4-based tracing for line coverage

## Coverage Metrics

### 1. Function Coverage
- List all exported functions per module
- Track function calls during test execution
- Report: `functions_tested / functions_total`

### 2. Test-to-Function Mapping
```json
{
  "config.sh": {
    "config_init": ["test_config.sh::test_init"],
    "config_load": ["test_config.sh::test_load", "test_e2e.sh::test_full_flow"],
    "config_validate": []  // Not tested\!
  }
}
```

### 3. Gap Analysis
- Functions with zero test coverage
- Functions with only happy-path coverage
- Modules with low overall coverage

## Implementation

### Coverage Script
```bash
./scripts/coverage.sh [--module config.sh] [--format json|text]
```

### Output
```
=== Coverage Report ===
Module            Functions  Tested  Coverage
config.sh              8       6     75%
github.sh             10       7     70%
build_state.sh        15      15    100%
...
TOTAL                 50      42     84%

Untested functions:
- config.sh: config_validate
- github.sh: gh_create_release, gh_upload_asset, gh_delete_release
```

## CI Integration
- Run after test suite
- Fail if coverage drops below threshold
- Generate JSON for trending

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse exported functions from all src/*.sh
- [ ] Track function calls during test runs
- [ ] Generate human-readable report
- [ ] Generate JSON for CI
- [ ] Flag untested functions
- [ ] Optional threshold enforcement","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:49:24.894461697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ov2","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
